name: Antas das Pedras Altas Trail
name_original: Antas das Pedras Altas — percurso circular (PR9 TVR)
description: Hike the moderate 6.0-mile Antas das Pedras Altas Trail to discover ancient megalithic monuments and panoramic views in Portugal's Algarve r
region_intro: Easy hiking trails around Cachopo offer a serene experience in the Serra do Caldeirão mountains of Portugal's Algarve region. The terrain is characterized by rolling hills and dense cork oak forests, providing a tranquil environment for outdoor exploration. Hikers can traverse dirt tracks and paths that wind through these landscapes, which feature gentle gradients and rich biodiversity. The area's schist and greywacke soils support varied flora, and panoramic views of the Algarve can be seen from higher points. Best easy hiking trails around Cachopo The most popular easy hiking route is Barranco do Velho — circular route (PR17), a 2.8 miles (4.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 19 minutes to complete. This route leads through cork forests and open hillsides, offering a peaceful walk. Another top favourite among local hikers is Masmorra Trail, an easy 3.4 miles (5.4 km) path. This trail passes ancient circular buildings and a Neolithic dolmen, providing expansive views of the surrounding landscape. Local hikers also love the Barranco das Lajes — circular route (PR3 SBA), a 3.4 miles (5.4 km) trail leading through rolling hills and traditional rural scenery, often completed in about 1 hour 35 minutes. Hiking around Cachopo is defined by rolling hills, cork oak forests, and panoramic views of the Algarve. The network offers easy hiking trails suitable for various ability levels, allowing for exploration of the region's natural beauty. The routes in Cachopo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 800 hikers have used komoot to explore Cachopo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are available around Cachopo?
A: There are over 15 easy hiking trails around Cachopo, offering a variety of routes through the Serra do Caldeirão mountains. In total, the region features 52 hiking tours, with 17 specifically categorized as easy.

Q: Are there any family-friendly easy hikes in the Cachopo area?
A: Yes, many of the easy trails are suitable for families. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1421609900/barranco-do-velho-circular-route-pr17" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco do Velho — circular route (PR17)&lt;/a&gt; are relatively short, at 2.8 miles (4.5 km), and lead through cork forests and open hillsides, providing a pleasant experience for all ages.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the easy hiking trails near Cachopo?
A: The trails around Cachopo, winding through cork oak forests and open hillsides, are generally dog-friendly. Always ensure your dog is under control and respect local wildlife and other hikers. It's advisable to check specific trail regulations if you encounter marked natural reserves.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes will I see on these easy hikes?
A: Easy hikes in Cachopo will immerse you in rolling hills and dense cork oak forests, characteristic of the Serra do Caldeirão. You'll traverse dirt tracks and paths, with opportunities to see varied flora like wild lavender and bee orchids in spring. Some routes also offer panoramic views of the Algarve, with the distant Atlantic Ocean.

Q: Are there any circular easy routes for hikers who prefer loops?
A: Yes, several easy circular routes are available. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1421609900/barranco-do-velho-circular-route-pr17" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco do Velho — circular route (PR17)&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1422448322/barranco-das-lajes-circular-route-pr3-sba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco das Lajes — circular route (PR3 SBA)&lt;/a&gt;, both offering engaging loops through the region's natural beauty.

Q: What historical or cultural points of interest can I discover on an easy hike?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1422364397/masmorra-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Masmorra Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for example, passes ancient circular buildings known as 'Casas Circulares' or 'Palheiros' and a Neolithic dolmen. The village of Cachopo itself offers a glimpse into traditional rural life with its whitewashed houses and the historic Santo Estevão Mother Church. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3423560" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa dos Cantoneiros de Barranco do Velho&lt;/a&gt;, a historical site near some routes.

Q: Is public transport available to reach the hiking areas around Cachopo?
A: Public transport options directly to the more remote hiking trailheads around Cachopo are limited. It is generally recommended to use a car to access the starting points of many trails in the Serra do Caldeirão, allowing for greater flexibility in exploring the region.

Q: Where can I find parking when planning an easy hike in Cachopo?
A: Parking is typically available in or near the villages that serve as starting points for the trails, such as Cachopo or Mealha. For specific trailheads like the Masmorra Trail, you can often find informal parking areas nearby. It's always a good idea to check the route details on komoot for specific parking recommendations.

Q: What is the best time of year to enjoy easy hiking in Cachopo?
A: The best time for easy hiking in Cachopo is during spring (March to May) when the undergrowth bursts with wild lavender and other wildflowers, and the weather is pleasantly mild. Autumn (September to November) also offers comfortable temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summers can be very hot, making early mornings or late afternoons preferable for hiking.

Q: Are there opportunities to see wildlife while hiking in Cachopo?
A: Yes, the Serra do Caldeirão is a haven for wildlife. Birdwatchers can spot woodland passerines and birds of prey such as the Short-toed Eagle, Goshawk, and Sparrowhawk. During winter, Griffon Vultures can also be seen. The tranquil environment of the cork oak forests provides a habitat for various species.

Q: What do other hikers say about the easy trails in Cachopo?
A: The easy trails in Cachopo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 200 reviews. Hikers often praise the quiet woodlands, the gentle gradients, and the opportunity to experience unspoiled nature away from the bustling coast.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to eat near the easy hiking routes?
A: While Cachopo is a small village, you can find local amenities there. For specific routes, you might find small establishments in nearby villages. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5522913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tia Bia Restaurant&lt;/a&gt; is a highlight in the area, offering a place to refuel after your hike.

Q: What are some shorter, easy walking routes around Cachopo?
A: For a shorter, easy walk, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1419875432/ameixial-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ameixial Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is approximately 3 miles (4.8 km) long. These routes are perfect for a quick stroll to enjoy the natural surroundings without a significant time commitment.

Q: What makes the easy hiking trails in Cachopo suitable for beginners?
A: The easy hiking trails in Cachopo are characterized by their gentle gradients and well-maintained dirt tracks, making them ideal for beginners. The routes typically avoid steep climbs and challenging terrain, allowing new hikers to comfortably enjoy the natural beauty of the Serra do Caldeirão.

Q: Are there any viewpoints on the easy trails offering panoramic views?
A: Yes, some easy trails lead to elevated points offering expansive vistas. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1422364397/masmorra-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Masmorra Trail&lt;/a&gt; includes a Neolithic dolmen on a hill with broad views. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4037493" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Alcaria do Cume and the Atlantic Ocean&lt;/a&gt; is a notable viewpoint in the region, accessible from various trails.
tips: Mealha is a small village in Tavira. Its main attraction is the rustic water fountain, located in the central part of the place, which still serves the local population. From here, several trails leave for the region.

The Antas da Valeira are two dolmens from the Chalcolithic period, located on an agricultural property, close to the Cromlech of Vale Maria do Meio and the megalithic complex of Almendres. Unfortunately, they have not been studied archaeologically and are in poor condition.

The Anta das Pedras Altas, also known as Anta de Mealha, is a megalithic monument in the municipality of Tavira, built at the end of the Neolithic or Chalcolithic. The place offers a unique panorama of the area. Important archaeological discoveries were made here in the 1970s, including pieces of chipped and polished stone.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The Antas das Pedras Altas Trail is a moderate hike that usually takes around 2 hours and 40 minutes to complete. This duration is for the full 9.65 km circular route.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Antas das Pedras Altas Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features varied terrain, starting with some initial steep uphill sections before descending through areas with umbrella pines and eucalyptus trees. You'll also traverse dry riverbeds. The route offers tranquil Algarve countryside with panoramic views, where natural sounds prevail.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: While rated as moderate, requiring good fitness, the 9.65 km circular route has mostly accessible paths. Some sections, like the initial uphill, can be steep. There's also a shorter, less steep 300-meter uphill path directly to the Anta das Pedras Altas, which might be more suitable for beginners or families looking for a shorter experience.

faqs.question: What historical or natural landmarks can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The most significant feature is the Anta das Pedras Altas, a unique megalithic monument from the Neolithic period. You might also pass by old round huts called 'palheiros' near the village of Mealha. Other notable points include the Mealha Fountain and Antas da Valeira.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail is located near the village of Mealha in the Algarve region. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, it's generally recommended to look for parking options within or on the outskirts of Mealha, as the circular route often starts and ends there.

faqs.question: Is the Antas das Pedras Altas Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally considered dog-friendly. However, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas where you might encounter wildlife or other hikers, and to carry water for them.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits required or entrance fees to hike the Antas das Pedras Altas Trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit the Antas das Pedras Altas Trail?
faqs.answer: The Algarve region generally has mild winters and hot summers. Spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) are often considered the best times to hike, offering pleasant temperatures and less crowded trails. Summers can be very hot, so if hiking then, start early in the morning.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Antas das Pedras Altas Trail is part of a network of paths. The route itself is known as the Percurso das Antas das Pedras Altas. It also intersects with sections of the Percurso Descoberta, Percurso do Vale das Hortas, and Percurso da Masmorra.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or facilities near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail is located near the village of Mealha. While specific cafes or facilities directly at the trailhead are not mentioned, you may find local establishments in Mealha or nearby towns for refreshments or amenities after your hike.

faqs.question: Is the Antas das Pedras Altas Trail a loop or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: The Antas das Pedras Altas Trail is a circular route, meaning it loops back to its starting point. This allows you to experience varied scenery without retracing your steps.
